raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Asserting a commitment to radical self-agency and refusal of external rescue or blame-shifting as the basis for behavioral change",
  "implicit_claims": ["Only the individual possesses the capacity to modify their own condition", "External agents or circumstances lack any decisive causal power over personal outcomes", "Change is therefore always within the scope of individual volition"],
  "coherence_assessment": "Logically consistent within a framework of absolute internal locus of control; the absolute phrasing creates no internal contradiction but renders the claim unfalsifiable from within its own premises",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The individual faces no insurmountable external constraints, possesses adequate resources and capabilities for self-alteration, and benefits from heightened personal accountability as a motivator",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Systemic barriers, structural inequalities, or dependencies on others are operative, such that the statement functions to obscure shared or external responsibility and induce unwarranted self-blame"
}
